differences.title: 1. RENDERING PHILOSOPHY
differences.leftDesc: Zero JS by default, hydrates only interactive components.
differences.rightDesc: Server-side and client-side rendering, full React ecosystem.

differences.title: 2. PERFORMANCE FOCUS
differences.leftDesc: Minimal client-side JavaScript leads to quicker initial loads.
differences.rightDesc: Code splitting, image optimization, prefetching for speed.

differences.title: 3. DEFAULT JS OUTPUT
differences.leftDesc: No JavaScript shipped to the browser unless explicitly needed.
differences.rightDesc: JavaScript for components is shipped and executed client-side.

differences.title: 4. SUPPORTED FRAMEWORKS
differences.leftDesc: Works with React, Vue, Svelte, Preact, and vanilla JS.
differences.rightDesc: Primarily built for and optimized around React.

differences.title: 5. API ROUTES
differences.leftDesc: Built-in support for fetching data in content pages.
differences.rightDesc: Robust API routes for building backend functionalities.

differences.title: 6. USE CASE STRENGTH
differences.leftDesc: Ideal for blogs, marketing sites, documentation, portfolios.
differences.rightDesc: Best for complex web applications, e-commerce, dashboards.

differences.title: 7. LEARNING CURVE
differences.leftDesc: Easier to grasp for static site generation needs.
differences.rightDesc: Requires understanding React and Next.js specific concepts.
